LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. KEVIN SINGER'S MOTION FOR APPROVAL OF THE DISTRIBUTION OF THE FUNDS FROM THE SALE OF THE PROPERTY 2) APPROVING AND SETTLING REFEREE'S FINAL REPORT AND ACCOUNTING 3) APPROVING FINAL COMPENSATION AND REIMBURSEMENT OF COSTS FOR REFERREE 4) DISCHARGING REFEREE FROM ASSINGNMENT 5) APPROVING AND RATIFYING ALL OF REFERREE'S OTHER ACTS 6) RETAINING JURISDICTION REGARDING REFEREE ESTATE AND APPOINTMENT IS GRANTED IN ITS ENTIRETY. JUDGE KAHN DISCLOSES THAT HE BELIEVES THAT AMY HARRINGTON IS MARRIED TO JUDGE GERARDO SANDOVAL, A JUDGE OF THE SAN FRANCISCO SUPERIOR COURT, BUT THAT HE IS ABLE TO DECIDE THIS MOTION FAIRLY AND IMPARTIALLY. PLAINTIFFS' OBJECTION TO THE FEES OF MR. RAMOS IS OVERRULED. IT WAS REASONABLE FOR MS. HARRINGTON TO HIRE COUNSEL AND THE FEES OF HER COUNSEL WERE REASONABLE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

Ntc Of Motion For Approval Of The Distribution Of The Funds From The Sale Of The Property
Off Calendar Jul-11-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. AMANDA L. EBEY, TEL. NO. 989-8070 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ESTHER JUAREZ, PLAINTIFF ALSO APPEARING IN COURT;
KEVIN SINGER, TEL. NO. 848-2984, COURT RECEIVER;
MR. JULIO RAMOS, ALSO PRESENT IN COURT

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. KEVIN SINGER'S Motion For Approval Of The Distribution Of The Funds From The Sale Of The Property 2) Approving and settling referee's Final report and accounting 3) Approving Final Compensation and Reimbursement of Costs for Referree 4) Discharging Referee from assingnment 5) Approving and ratifying all of referree's other acts 6) Retaining Jurisdiction regarding referee estate and appointment is Granted in its entirety. Judge Kahn discloses that he believes that Amy Harrington is married to Judge Gerardo Sandoval, a judge of the San Francisco Superior Court, but that he is able to decide this motion fairly and impartially. Plaintiffs' objection to the fees of Mr. Ramos is overruled. It was reasonable for Ms. Harrington to hire counsel and the fees of her counsel were reasonable. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/HEK)

Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Off Calendar Mar-09-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

Amanda L. Ebey, Esq. appeared with plaintiff Thelma S. Montes, who was placed under oath and examined.
Ms. Ebey appeared on behalf of plaintiffs Esther Juarez, Leticia Martinez, Eduardo Juarez, III, Alcia Payne and Carlos Garcia, who were not present at today's hearing.
No appearance by defendant Jorge Martin de Anda.
Default was entered against defendant on December 3, 2009.
Doe defendants were dismissed on March 3, 2010.
Lis Pendens was filed on March 3, 2010.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiffs Esther Juarez, Leticia Martinez, Thelma S. Montes, Eduardo Juarez, III, Alicia Payne and Carlos Garcia, and against the Defaulted Defendant Jorge Martin de Anda as follows:

(1) The real property located at 1051 Shotwell Street, San Francisco, CA shall be partitioned by sale, and the net proceeds be divided between each of the owners;
(2) Counsel for plaintiffs shall submit to the Court the name of the proposed referee no later than March 23, 2010:
(3) Costs of this partition action are awarded costs & attoney fees, and that said costs of suit shall become a lien on defendant Jorge Martin de Anda's proceeds from the sale of the property and
(4) The Court shall retain jurisdiction over this matter, which is continued to August 10, 2010 @ 9:00 a.m in Department 218 for final judgment.

Interlocutory Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-22-2010 Continued To Apr-16-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Off Calendar Mar-02-2010 Continued To Mar-09-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 218
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

Amanda L. Ebey, Esq. appeared with plaintiffs Thelma Montes, Alicia Payne and Leticia Martinez.
Plaintiff Thelma Montes was placed under oath but did not testify.
No appearance by defendant Jore Martin de Anda.
Default was entered against defendant on December 3, 2009.
Doe defendants have not been dismissed.

Plaintiff has not filed a Lis Pendens in this action as required by Code of Civil Procedure Section 872.250. Good cause appearing, this matter is continued to March 9, 2009 @ 9:00 a.m. in Department 218.
